self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can present and enforce downstream Runtime approvals more truthfully when opening legality depends on the live current head, not only on artifact-local status text.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP bounded result adds one shared current-head legality guard for Runtime approval surfaces and downstream openers.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this slice, historical Runtime follow-up, record, proof, and capability-boundary artifacts could still advertise approval and some openers could still rely on local status fields even after the live current head had moved to a later stage.
self_improvement.expected_effect: The host detail layer and approval boundary now block this stale-status overstatement class without redesigning broader stale-status handling.

decision.rationale: This bounded legality slice adds immediate shared truth value without broadening into generic stale-status repair.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
